notes: Two visual regressions caught in the v1.0.47 install: the ASA wordmark was rendering oversized and horizontally-stretched on the login / onboarding / admin pages, and the Taskbar icon was still showing soft edges at non-100% DPI. ### Wordmark stretch: tight master + aspect-safe CSS `static/branding/wordmark-master-transparent.png` had survived from a much earlier session as a **1536Ã—1024** canvas with the wordmark scaled into a sub-region â€” internal aspect â‰ˆ 2.07:1, natural aspect 2.95:1. Rendering it at `width:200px;height:auto` produced a 200Ã—133 box with the visible glyphs vertically squashed (A's looked too wide). Replaced with a copy of the v1.0.47 Copilot-source `wordmark-tight-on-dark.png` (434Ã—147) so the aspect inside matches the canvas itself. Separately, `dashboard.html` sidebar CSS used `width:100%;max-height:40px;height:auto`, which is *mathematically* a stretch: the browser computes width from `100%` (â‰ˆ180px in a 220px sidebar) then independently clamps height to 40, producing a 180Ã—40 box (4.5:1) for a 2.95:1 source â€” bitmap stretches to fill. Switched to `max-width:100%;max-height:40px;width:auto;height:auto` so the longer of the two constraints drives a uniform scale. Login / onboarding / admin templates (`templates/login.html`, `templates/onboarding.html`, `templates/admin.html`) updated to the same `max-width / max-height / width:auto / height:auto` pattern, defensively â€” if a future master file ever reverts to a non-natural canvas, the markup won't visibly stretch. Inline `style="width:200px;height:auto"` removed from the `` tags so the stylesheet rule wins. `tray-app/src/App.tsx` splash uses the same constraint pattern (`maxWidth:200,maxHeight:70,width:'auto',height:'auto'`) for consistency with the web templates. ### Taskbar icon: tight S crop + 10% padding + multi-step Lanczos v1.0.47's icon was tight against the canvas edge, so at 16Ã—16 (notification overflow, jump-list small) the green S touched all four borders and Windows' shell-rendering gave it a smudged read. Regenerated via `scripts/regen_icon.py`: - **Source:** `static/branding/wordmark-on-dark@3x.png` (1200Ã—426). The S glyph is isolated by an HSV-style green-dominance test (G â‰¥ 60 AND Gâˆ’R â‰¥ 40 AND Gâˆ’B â‰¥ 40) so the dark fold panels survive while the white A's drop out. Bounding box yields a 324Ã—363 raw glyph. - **Padding:** 10% on each side â†’ padded square is 436Ã—436. Means the visible glyph never reaches the icon's outer pixel ring even at 16Ã—16. - **Downscale:** repeated halving with Lanczos (each step integrates a clean 2Ã—2 with the full kernel) until within 2Ã— of target, then a final Lanczos to the exact size. Multi-step beats a single big resize for thin-edge artwork. - **Sharpen:** mild `UnsharpMask` on frames â‰¤ 48 px (radius 0.6â€“0.8, percent 90â€“110, threshold 2). Recovers perceived edge contrast lost in resampling without halo artifacts. - **Frames:** 16, 20, 24, 30, 32, 36, 40, 48, 64, 96, 128, 256 â€” one for each Windows DPI scale that actually renders something. - **PNG-encoded ICO frames** so each per-frame bitmap in `icon.ico` is byte-for-byte the bitmap Pillow produced (no double-resample inside Pillow's BMP+AND-mask path). ### Files touched - `static/branding/wordmark-master-transparent.png` â€” replaced bloated 1536Ã—1024 master with the tight 434Ã—147 Copilot-source artwork - `static/css/components.css` â€” sidebar wordmark CSS (`.sidebar .logo-row .wordmark-asa`) - `templates/login.html`, `templates/onboarding.html`, `templates/admin.html` â€” aspect-safe wordmark constraints + remove inline width/height - `tray-app/src/App.tsx` â€” splash wordmark uses max-width / max-height - `tray-app/src-tauri/icons/{32x32,64x64,128x128,128x128@2x,icon}.png`, `tray-app/src-tauri/icons/icon.ico` â€” regenerated from the green-isolated S with padding - `scripts/regen_icon.py` â€” new icon-regen tool (S isolation + multi-step Lanczos + unsharp) ### Auto-updater note Same pipeline as v1.0.47 â€” `scripts/release.ps1` sets `TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY` + `TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PASSWORD` via Win32 `SetEnvironmentVariableW` so tauri-cli's auto-sign step finds the key without falling through to a stdin passphrase prompt. Manifest mirror-pushed to `cjml34483/ASA-releases` and `cjml34483/TradeSpace-releases`.
